other_observers: none; I found and ID'ed the bird
specific_location: on Hoy Mountain, in the location of my two previous sightings
description: the bird was a grouse significantly smaller than Blue Grouse seen immediately after the observation. The overall color was a gray-brown, with much less of a blue-gray color than the Blue Grouse, and more of a browner look. It also appeared a little paler in flight. When seen in flight I was able to see the bold black subterminal band on the otherwise brownish tail. The tail was rounded in shape. In the very brief look I got of the bird on the ground I was able to get a quick glimpse of a wispy crest, reminiscent of a Northern Cardinal.  When the bird was flushed it flew low over the ground, and the wingbeats were very quiet.Behaviors: I saw one bird that I flushed from the ground, and heard another a few minutes later.Call: a bird was heard drumming, sounding like a distant bird taking off, an increasingly fast series of very low-pitched "whump"s. This may or may not have been the bird originally flushed.Plumage: adult
